title: The Log Is The Agent - Ishaan Sehgal, Omnara
summary: Ishaan Sehgal argues that agent identity should reside in durable session logs rather than in models, tools, or compute infrastructure. He draws an analogy to video game save files to illustrate how logs enable portability, durability, and continuity across different models and runtimes. He contends that as models commoditize, the differentiator shifts to infrastructure that keeps logs independent from model providers.
writeup: Ishaan Sehgal of Omnara argues that an agent's identity lives in its durable session logs, not in the model, tools, or compute that run it. He uses an analogy to video game save files to show how append-only logs provide portability, durability, and continuity across different models and runtimes. The claim: as models commoditize, the differentiator moves to infrastructure that keeps logs independent from model providers, with implications for orchestration and observability.
